fields.description:
## Description
Wire the code_health metric specs to read via the analyzer (LOC/complexity/duplication) instead of scanning `src/rebar/**/*.py` and `.github/module-size-limit.txt`. Re-express the module-size cap as an OPTIONAL policy over the analyzer LOC signal (from `[code_health]` config), and make missing/unconfigured analyzer render honest Unavailable. Does NOT touch the CI module-size GATE (separate governance).

## Per-metric post-migration semantics (resolving the blocking findings)
- `module_size_distribution`, `oversized_module_count`: computed from `AnalyzerResult.loc` (per-file LOC) + the config `size_cap`/`size_near_fraction`. Currently `oversized_module_count` is a seed stub (compute=_no_data_yet); this ticket gives it a real compute.
- `module_size_trend`: reads the TIME SERIES from the existing snapshot store (`.rebar/metrics-snapshots.ndjson` via `rebar.metrics.snapshot.read_snapshots`), NOT a single scan. Input = stored per-snapshot `max_loc`/`over_cap_count` records (written by the scc-based snapshot path); output = an ordered list of `{ts, value}` over the `[since, until]` window; Unavailable when no snapshots in range.
- `cap_change_events`: this tracks edits to `.github/module-size-limit.txt` — a REBAR-GOVERNANCE artifact with no client analogue. DECISION: REMOVE it from the portable code_health registry (deregister the spec). It may survive as a rebar-only opt-in guarded on the file existing, but it is NOT part of the client-facing analyzer stack. State the removal explicitly in the changelog note.

## Review-driven corrections
- `analyzer.py` is created by 3b30 (recorded depends_on edge) — reference, do not re-create; adapters live in `analyzers/`.
- Cap value comes from `[code_health]` config (e7a0, recorded via the adapter chain).
- Existing tests pin the OLD limit-file behavior: `tests/unit/test_git_metrics.py` and `tests/unit/test_git_metrics_heldout.py` (and `tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py`) MUST be updated for the new source / cap_change_events removal.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] module_size_distribution / oversized_module_count / module_size_trend read via analyzer + snapshot store + config; NO `src/rebar` or `.github/module-size-limit.txt` reference remains in the metric readers.
- [ ] `cap_change_events` spec deregistered from the portable registry (documented).
- [ ] No analyzer configured -> Unavailable; regression test asserts NO `{count:0,max_loc:0}` authoritative value and NO leaked errno reason.
- [ ] git-delta (churn/refactor_ratio) + agent_process metrics unchanged; existing git_metrics tests updated to match.

## Verification
- [ ] Regression test (foreign repo -> honest Unavailable, not fabricated zeros); updated test_git_metrics(.heldout) green; full in-repo `rebar metrics` green for unaffected metrics.

## Dependencies: 3b30, 18f9, 21fb, 3ba0 (recorded depends_on edges).
## Files likely touched: src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py, src/rebar/metrics/registry.py, src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py (consume), tests/unit/test_git_metrics.py, tests/unit/test_git_metrics_heldout.py, tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py
## Estimated scope: M-L
